note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to investigate the effects of prolactin stimulation on facial withdrawal threshold in mice as a preclinical headache model. The goal is to determine if prolactin administration affects the response to pain stimuli.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A digital caliper measures the inner diameter of a piece of tubing.",
    "A gas-tight, cemented needle microsyringe is prepared for use.",
    "Anesthesia is administered to a mouse using isoflurane.",
    "The mouse is restrained and anesthetized before receiving a prolactin injection.",
    "The facial withdrawal threshold is measured at various time points after injection."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Prolactin solution",
    "Microsyringe",
    "Isotonic saline",
    "Anesthesia machine",
    "Mice"
  ],

    "Microsyringe",
    "Anesthesia machine",
    "Restraint device",
    "Graphical data analysis software"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [

    "Injection volume (5 μg)",
    "Time intervals between measurements (BL, 1h, 3h, 5h, 24h, 48h, 72h, 10 days)"
  ],

    "Preparation of equipment and materials",
    "Administration of anesthesia and prolactin injection",
    "Measurement of facial withdrawal threshold",
    "Data collection and analysis"
  ],
